title: Align corruption-handling policy across bin/init.js#countFrameworkHistory and backlog seeder
description:
Follow-up from the TASK-017 reviewer audit. After TASK-017 landed, the seeder (`src/backlog-seeder.js#readAllTasksSync`) propagates JSON.parse errors on corrupt task files (per AC3 ‚Äî corruption surfaces so the user fixes the bytes rather than silently re-minting duplicates). However, `bin/init.js#countFrameworkHistory` ‚Äî the archive-prompt peek that runs a few lines earlier in the same `runInit` invocation ‚Äî still silently swallows the same class of error and treats the corrupt file as framework history.

**Consequence**: during a single `runInit` invocation, a corrupt `tasks/TASK-NNN.json` silently inflates the archive prompt count (user sees 'Detected 18 pre-existing tickets'), user answers Y, archive runs (with its own catch in `archiveFrameworkHistory`), then the seeder fires and throws ‚Äî leaving the user with a half-archived repo + a raised exception. The seeder's 'surface corruption immediately' intent is undermined by the earlier swallow.

**The in-code comment self-justifies the swallow as 'consistent with archive'**, but the seeder's AC3 trumps that consistency rule. The right move is to align the peek with the seeder.

**Two options for the IMPL dev to pick from:**
- **(a) Align countFrameworkHistory to throw on parse error** ‚Äî matches AC3 policy, surfaces corruption at the earliest possible point (BEFORE the archive prompt), gives the user a clear actionable error. Recommended.
- **(b) Document the deliberate asymmetry at both call sites** ‚Äî keeps the swallow but adds prose at countFrameworkHistory + at the seeder explaining why the two readers diverge intentionally. Weaker.

**Plus two LOW doc-polish items from the same TASK-017 review:**
- `src/backlog-seeder.js#readAllTasksSync` docstring still says 'Duplicated intentionally ‚Äî the orchestrator forbade widening task-store's public API just for this one read.' After AC7 that's now actively misleading (the regex is DRY-imported now). 1-line fix.
- `src/backlog-seeder.js:362` silent-ignore of unknown slugs (`if (!templates) continue; // Unknown use case slug ‚Äî silently ignore.`) matches AC2's design lock but a future maintainer might think it's unintentional. Extend the inline comment to reference AC2's design lock.
acceptance_criteria: `bin/init.js#countFrameworkHistory` no longer silently swallows JSON.parse failures from task files ‚Äî either it throws (option a) or the deliberate swallow is documented at both call sites (option b). A regression test pins whichever choice is made.

If option (a) is chosen, the throw surfaces BEFORE the archive prompt fires (no partial-archive scenario possible). Test: pre-seed a corrupt task file in a tmp repo, run init in created branch, assert the error surfaces with the file name before any prompter call.
`src/backlog-seeder.js#readAllTasksSync` docstring is updated to reflect post-AC7 reality (uses imported TASK_FILENAME_RE, not duplicated).
`src/backlog-seeder.js:362` (the `// Unknown use case slug ‚Äî silently ignore.` line) extends to reference TASK-017 AC2's design lock so the deliberate silent-ignore is unambiguous.

comments.author: orchestrator
comments.body: Created from the TASK-017 reviewer audit (verdict PASS, 1 MEDIUM + 2 LOWs). The MEDIUM is a real policy divergence between countFrameworkHistory (silent swallow) and the seeder (propagate per TASK-017 AC3) ‚Äî same module pair, same workflow invocation, but opposite policies. Reviewer recommended option (a) ‚Äî align countFrameworkHistory to throw. The 2 LOWs are documentation-only polish picked up alongside since they touch the same files.
